Frrn Polrrrcel PnrcrrcBs C ovrvussroN
P.O. Box ll07 . "t2ti J Strcet . Sacrarnenro. CA f.ittll-0tt07
(916) 32:--5660. Fax (916) -122-0tttt6
August 30, 2000
William A. Silva
Woodman Development Company, Inc.
201 Monterey-Salinas Highway, Suite A
Salinas, California 93908
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No.I-00-094
Dear Mr. Silva:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r The Act requires the Commission to provide
written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's
authorized representative. (Section 8311a0); Regulation 183290)(1), enclosed.) Formal
written advice is the application of the law to a particular set of facts provided by the requestor
and has the purpose of conferring immunity on the requestor. (Section 831 14; Regulation
L8329(b)(7).) As such, the Commission does not respond to requests relating to past conduct.
(Regulation 18329(b)(8XA).) The Commission may decline to provide informal assistance in
such a situation as well. (Regulation 18329(c)@XA).)
Based on information included in your correspondence, we conclude that advice on your
question would necessarily relate to past conduct. Therefore, the Commission declines to issue
advice on this matter. However, our decision to decline to provide formal or informal advice is
not a determination that we have reached a conclusion regarding the question posed in your
request.
' Goue.nm.nt Code sections 8 1000 - 9 10 15. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections I 8 I 09 -
18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. I-00-094
Page 2
For your information, we have enclosed the FPPC document entitled, ,,Can I Vote?
Conflicts of Interest Overview." We hope this may be useful to you with future decisions in
which you may participate as a public official. If you have any other questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
